purpose: Side panel showing a sorted chronological timeline of task events for the current task plus a History section listing past tasks. Builds events on every render from `task.started_at`, each agent's started_at/completed_at (label varies by status), and `task.completed_at`. Sorts by ISO timestamp. Auto-scrolls to bottom on visibility change or when events.length changes. Two sub-components: TimelineEntry (dot + time + label + agent title) and HistoryEntry (dot + name + meta which is duration when both timestamps exist, otherwise overall_status).

exports.name: TimelinePanel
exports.signature: { status: { active_task, agents, history }, visible: boolean, onClose: () => void }

gotchas: events array is rebuilt on EVERY render, not memoized — for swarms with hundreds of agents this is O(n log n) per render.
History is reversed in-place via `[...history].reverse()` — original array is not mutated, but each render allocates a new copy.
Auto-scroll uses requestAnimationFrame inside a useEffect; if the body element re-mounts at the same time, the scroll target may be the old node.
patterns: Status-to-label mapping inline in the build loop (`a.status === 'failed' ? 'Agent failed' : 'Agent completed'`).
Event keys combine timestamp + index to disambiguate co-located events.
conventions: Section labels (.timeline-section-label) separate Current Task from History; an empty state renders centered when both arrays are empty.
